data.title: UK construction activity decline unexpectedly accelerates in February
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Activity in the UK's construction sector declined for the fourteenth month in a row, S&P Global reported on Thursday.

The seasonally adjusted S&P Global UK construction purchasing managers' index posted 44.5 in February, down from a seven-month high of 46.4 points and remaining below the 50-point mark separating growth from contraction.

February's reading was below FXStreet-cited expectations of a rise to 47.9 points.

"A sharper downturn in house building was the main factor behind the setback for UK construction activity in February, following some signs of stabilisation at the start of 2026," S&P Global Market Intelligence's Economics Director Tim Moore commented. "Total industry activity has decreased in each month since January 2025 and the latest decline was faster than seen on average over this period.

"The reduction in output was largely due to sluggish demand conditions, but some firms also noted that exceptionally wet weather had disrupted construction projects."

However, Moore added: "Construction companies were hopeful of a turnaround in business activity over the year ahead, with optimism levels hitting a 14-month high in February.

"This was often linked to forthcoming new projects in the infrastructure and energy sectors, as well as projected improvements in broader economic conditions."

Released on Wednesday, the HCOB services PMI business activity index registered 53.9 points in February, down from 54.0 in January, and in line with the flash estimate.

The HCOB composite PMI output index came in at 53.7 points, the same as in January, but below the 53.9 flash estimate.

Released on Monday, the headline HCOB eurozone manufacturing PMI reading for February was 51.7 points, down from 51.8 in January, and underperforming against the flash reading of 52.0 points.

The construction PMI report is compiled by S&P Global from survey responses by a panel of around 150 construction companies. Last month's data was collected between February 10 and 26.

By Emma Curzon, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

Crude tanker reports suspected hull breach after blast near Iraq port
data.description:
LONDON, March 5 (Reuters) - The Bahamas flagged crude oil tanker Sonangol Namibe's hull was likely to have been breached after being hit by a blast while anchored near Iraq's Khor al Zubair port, U.S. representative company Sonangol Marine Services said on Thursday.

The vessel was approached by an unknown small vessel at 0120 local time on March 5.

"The small boat approached the port side of the tanker and a short time later a loud bang was heard," Sonangol Marine Services said in a statement.

"The crew reports that a port ballast tank is losing water which suggests some form of hull breach but the ship remains stable and safely afloat."

The vessel was not carrying cargo and there were no reports of pollution, the company added.

The tanker had a contract with Iraq's state oil marketer SOMO and was heading to an Iraqi terminal to load 80,000 METRIC tons of Iraqi fuel, an Iraqi energy source and a shipping agent said.

Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Thursday that they had hit a U.S. tanker in the northern part of the Gulf and the vessel was on fire.

(Reporting by Jonathan Saul and Ahmed Rasheed, Editing by Louise Heavens)

China's JD.com misses quarterly revenue estimates
data.description:
March 5 (Reuters) - China's JD.com missed market estimates for quarterly revenue on Thursday, in a sign that stiff competition and waning benefits from government subsidies were eating into demand at the e-commerce giant.

The company reported revenue of 352.3 billion yuan ($51.12 billion) in the fourth quarter ended December, compared with analysts' average estimate of 353.86 billion yuan, according to data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 6.8918 Chinese yuan renminbi)

(Reporting by Deborah Sophia in Bengaluru; Editing by Himani Sarkar)

data.title: Major Shareholder Investor Makes New Purchases in EQT — Now for SEK 108.5 Million
data.description:
Major shareholder, the investment company Investor, has reported new insider purchases in the private equity firm EQT.

Two transactions took place on March 3-4, resulting in Investor increasing its holding by 396,538 shares. The total purchase amount reached SEK 108.5 million, with an average price of SEK 273.73 per share.

This information is revealed in the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority's insider registry.

According to the ownership service Holdings, Investor owned 14.63 percent of the capital and votes in EQT before these latest transactions were registered.

Earlier this week, Investor reported purchases in EQT between February 27 and March 2 totaling SEK 319 million.

U.S. Economic Data Boosts Global Market Sentiment, Middle East Conflict Casts Shadow
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff


Global markets showed resilience even as the U.S. Senate green-lit the U.S. attacks against Iran amid an escalating Middle East conflict.

Oil prices remained elevated at above $83 a barrel, but stronger-than-expected economic signals from the ISM services index and private ADP jobs data pushed U.S. equities higher at Wednesday's close. Asian stocks picked up the rally--South Korea's Kospi shot up close to 10% a day after recording its worst-ever session--though U.S. futures edged down in early European trade.

The data, paired with inflation concerns arising from the Middle East conflict, reduced expectations of a near-term interest rate cut. U.S Treasury yields rose to a three-week high in Asian trade and the dollar strengthened further in response. Investors' insight into U.S. economic health will be further aided by non-farm payroll data set for release Friday.


--Oil extended gains as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran entered its sixth day. In early trading, Brent crude rose 3.1% to $83.89 a barrel, while WTI was up 2.5% at $73.53 a barrel. The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has trapped roughly a fifth of the oil the world consumes daily, forcing some producers to start curtailing production. The "punch to price will entirely depend of how long this continues," says Bjarne Schieldrop, chief commodities analyst at SEB Research. "Beyond those 7-12 days, that is when rubber will meet the road more explicitly."


--Gold prices rose in early trading despite a stronger U.S. dollar, as the widening Middle East conflict boosted demand for safe havens. In early trading, futures in New York were up 0.8% to $5,173.50 a troy ounce. "The market is keeping one eye on the impact of rising energy prices on inflation," analysts at ANZ said. "This has already seen traders scale back their bets on further interest rate cuts."


--U.S. futures pointed lower in early European trade. Futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.4%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.5%. Nasdaq futures also fell by 0.5%, though shares in artificial-intelligence cloud provider Broadcom surged 6.3% after earnings.


--Asian equities rebounded on Thursday after the historic selloff in the previous session. South Korean equities followed their worst day on record with a sharp recovery, the Kospi index rose 9.6% thanks to sharp rises in chip stocks, with AI memory maker SK Hynix climbing 11%. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was last up 0.4%. Strong U.S. economic data overnight supported risk appetite despite the Middle East conflict remaining uncertain.

China's Shanghai Composite Index gained 0.6% after the country set the 2026 GDP growth target at 4.5%-5.0%, signaling a pivot to quality over quantity. Market reaction to China's high-level policy meeting, where it set growth targets for the next five years, was fairly muted as there were no surprises in the readout.


--European indexes slid at the open as stocks threatened to reverse Wednesday's gains. The Italian FTSE MIB is the sharpest early faller, down 0.7% as banks that weigh heavily on the index slipped. Payment company Nexi fell by close to 15%. Germany's DAX was down 0.5%--after rising over 1.7% Wednesday--as healthcare and energy stocks dragged. Miners nudged London's FTSE 100 down 0.3%, while airlines continued to fall amid the continuing conflict in the Middle East. In Paris, the CAC 40 was down 0.6% as luxuries slipped, with LVMH falling 1.4%. Spain's IBEX 35 slipped 0.5% as banks and construction stocks dragged, though energy stocks in the index gained--Repsol climbed 2.7%.


--The dollar rose as strong U.S. economic data and the potential for higher inflation caused by the Middle East conflict has prompted markets to scale back bets for further Fed rate cuts. The market is now only fully pricing in one rate cut this year by September, LSEG data show. The DXY dollar index rose 0.3% to 99.004.


--The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield hit a three-week high of 4.131% in Asian trade before retreating as investors kept focus on the inflationary impact of the war. The two-year Treasury yield rose 1.3 basis points to 3.554%; the 10-year yield climbed 3.5 basis points to 4.116%; and the 30-year yield rose 4.1 basis points to 4.757%, according to Tradeweb.

Eurozone government bond yields rose in early trade, reversing the previous day's moves. The 10-year German Bund yield climbed 4 basis points to 2.784%, according to Tradeweb. Ten-year Spanish and French bond yields rose slightly more ahead of those countries' bond auctions.


--Bitcoin eased 1.5% to $72,248 after reaching a one-month high of $74,049 Wednesday on improved market sentiment.


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-05-26 0455ET

Galderma Group AG Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Galderma Group AG reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was USD 5,207 million compared to USD 4,410 million a year ago. Net income was USD 613 million compared to USD 231 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 2.6 compared to USD 0.97 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 2.58 compared to USD 0.97 a year ago.

GAC Toyota launches presales for new electric sedan
data.description:
BEIJING, March 5 (Reuters) - GAC Toyota has started pre-sales of its new D-segment bZ7 electric sedan with pre-order prices from 156,800 yuan ($22,759), the company, a joint venture between Guangzhou Automobile Group and Toyota, said on Thursday. 

($1 = 6.8895 Chinese yuan renminbi)

(Reporting by Yukun Zhang and Ryan Woo; Editing by Emelia Sithole-Matarise)

data.title: China's Estun Automation to price HK listing at low end of range, targets $191 million raise
data.description:
March 5 (Reuters) - China's Estun Automation will determine the price of its listing in Hong Kong at the bottom end of its offer range, the industrial robot maker said on Thursday, seeking to raise HK$1.49 billion ($190.54 million) from the share sale.

The Shenzhen-listed company has offered 96.8 million H shares for the listing at HK$15.36 per share, below the maximum offer price of HK$17 apiece announced last week.

Estun was among a string of companies to seek listings in Hong Kong after the Lunar New Year holiday break, extending a brisk start of share launches in 2026.

Hong Kong's stock market logged its strongest start to a year since 2021, with IPOs and second listings raising about $5.5 billion in January, the most since $7.6 billion in January 2021, LSEG data showed earlier in February.

Estun last week said funds from the offering would support manufacturing capacity, research, development, and overseas growth initiatives for the company.

The firm is expected to disclose the final offer price on Friday, with its shares slated to debut on the Hong Kong stock exchange on March 9.

($1 = 7.8199 Hong Kong dollars)

(Reporting by Nikita Maria Jino in Bengaluru; Editing by Maju Samuel)

Broadcom beats expectations thanks to the AI boom
data.description:
Broadcom posted better-than-expected Q1 results, supported by strong growth in its artificial intelligence-related business. The group reported adjusted EPS of $2.05 and revenue of $19.31bn, above analysts' estimates. Revenue rose 29% year-on-year for the period ended February 1.

Demand for AI technologies strongly boosted activity. AI-related revenue more than doubled to $8.4bn, up 106%. According to Chief Executive Officer Hock Tan, the momentum is being driven by demand for custom AI accelerators and networking solutions for data centers. The semiconductor division generated $12.52bn in revenue, while the infrastructure software business reached $6.80bn.

For Q2, Broadcom forecasts revenue of around $22bn, well above the $20.56bn expected by the market, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 68%. The group also announced a new share buyback program of up to $10bn and expects to generate $10.7bn in AI semiconductor revenue for the current quarter.

The stock was up nearly 0.5% in after-hours trading.
